title: Exploring the roles of ribosomal peptides in prokaryote-phage interactions through deep learning-enabled metagenome mining
objective: Develop deep learning method to identify RiPPs from ocean metagenomes and explore their ecological roles in prokaryote-phage interactions through metatranscriptomic analysis and host-phage network construction
problem_statement: Most RiPP ecological functions remain largely unexplored. Traditional genome mining methods fail on highly fragmented metagenomic data from complex microbiota. Previous metagenome approaches were limited to studying culturable microbes. Discovery potential of RiPPs from unculturable ocean microbiota with tens of millions of viruses remains untapped

research_gap.discovery_challenge: RiPP identification from highly fragmented metagenomes is limited by traditional PTM enzyme detection methods
research_gap.ecological_understanding: RiPP ecological functions in prokaryote-phage interactions remain undefined
research_gap.ocean_microbiome: Home to 10:1 virus-to-microbial cell ratio leading to dynamic prokaryote-phage battles, yet remains chemically underexplored

methodology.innovation: TrRiPP - deep learning method for RiPP discovery from metagenomes

components: Transformer encoder (for attention maps on amino acid sequences)
trripp_deep_learning_model.training_data: Dataset from NeuRiPP and DeepRiPP supplemented with manual curation

binary_classification.comparison: Significantly improved over NeuRiPP and DeepRiPP (P<0.0005)

ripp_subclass_classification.comparison: Superior to DeepRiPP across all metrics

trripp_performance.generalization: Performed well across entire range of sequence similarities (10-100%)
trripp_performance.external_validation: Tested on external datasets and bacterial complete genomes

antismash_performance.complete_genomes: 36.9% to 77.1% of antiSMASH-identified precursors recovered by TrRiPP
antismash_performance.additional_discovery: Hundreds to thousands of precursors not identified by antiSMASH
antismash_performance.novel_classes: Enabled prediction of autoinducing peptides and graspetides

fragmented_genomes_advantage.methodology: Artificially fragmented 100 bacterial complete genomes to simulate metagenomic contigs
fragmented_genomes_advantage.antismash_result: Only 1 of 46 RiPP precursors recovered from fragmented versions
fragmented_genomes_advantage.trripp_result: 109 of 217 predicted precursors recovered from fragmented versions
fragmented_genomes_advantage.improvement: Demonstrates critical advantage for metagenome analysis

ripp_discovery_statistics.families_identified: Over 8000 RiPP families from ocean metagenomes
ripp_discovery_statistics.rarefaction_analysis: Predicted potential of ~27,563 RiPP families when including engineered and other environmental systems
ripp_discovery_statistics.context: MIBiG3 contains only 254 documented RiPP families (0.9-1.6% of predicted ocean microbiome potential)

ecological_function_analysis.co_expression_patterns: High prevalence of antiphage defense-related and phage-related protein families co-expressed with RiPP families
ecological_function_analysis.evidence_for_phage_interaction: Putative association between RiPPs and phage infection
comparative_findings: Enterocins AAR-71, AAR-74 show antiphage activity
Anthracyclines from Streptomyces provide chemical defense against phages
Aminoglycosides provide chemical defense against phages

host_phage_interaction_network.findings: Complex connectivities linking RiPP biosynthesis to prokaryote-phage interactions and coevolution

ecological_significance.hypothesis: RiPPs function as antiphage defense mechanisms similar to anthracyclines and aminoglycosides
ecological_significance.virus_to_microbial_ratio: 10:1 in ocean (high selective pressure for defense mechanisms)
ecological_significance.adaptive_function: RiPPs potentially enable microbes to flourish in highly competitive ocean environments
ecological_significance.coevolution: Evidence of RiPP-phage coevolutionary arms race

research_impact.methodology: TrRiPP addresses critical limitation of traditional genome mining on fragmented data
research_impact.discovery_scope: Unlocks massive hidden chemical diversity in ocean microbiome
research_impact.ecological_insights: First systematic investigation of RiPP biosynthetic potential and ecological roles at global scale
research_impact.biotechnology_implications: Access to vast new RiPP families for drug discovery
research_impact.microbe_ecology: Demonstrates ecological importance of secondary metabolites in prokaryote-phage interactions

validation_metrics.validation_approach: Examined sequence features and genomic neighborhoods
validation_metrics.results: 43.0-91.9% of predicted precursors co-localized with PTM enzymes or RiPP-related genes
validation_metrics.amino_acid_conservation: Over 99% of precursors carried intrinsic amino acids required for PTM reactions
